act.title: The Indian Penal Code, 1860
act.description: A general penal code for India.

sections.title: Title and extent of operation of the Code
sections.content: This Act shall be called the Indian Penal Code, and shall extend to the whole of India except the State of Jammu and Kashmir.

sections.title: Punishment of offences committed within India
sections.content: Every person shall be liable to punishment under this Code and not otherwise for every act or omission contrary to the provisions thereof, of which he shall be guilty within India.

subsections.content: Every person shall be liable to punishment under this Code.

sections.title: Murder
sections.content: Except in the cases hereinafter excepted, culpable homicide is murder, if the act by which the death is caused is done with the intention of causing death, or if it is done with the intention of causing such bodily injury as the offender knows to be likely to cause the death of the person to whom the harm is caused.

subsections.content: Culpable homicide is not murder if the offender, whilst deprived of the power of self-control by grave and sudden provocation, causes the death of the person who gave the provocation.

Culpable homicide is not murder if the offender, in the exercise in good faith of the right of private defence of person or property, exceeds the power given to him by law and causes the death of the person against whom he is exercising such right of defence.

sections.title: Punishment for murder
sections.content: Whoever commits murder shall be punished with death, or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.

sections.title: Assault or criminal force to woman with intent to outrage her modesty
sections.content: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any woman, intending to outrage or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby outrage her modesty, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.

sections.title: Rape
sections.content: A man is said to commit rape if he has sexual intercourse with a woman under circumstances falling under any of the six following descriptions. Sixth — With or without her consent, when she is under sixteen years of age.

amendments.title: The Criminal Law (Amendment) Act, 2013
amendments.description: Amendment following the Nirbhaya case — strengthened provisions on sexual offences.

actions.old_content: Sixth — With or without her consent, when she is under sixteen years of age.
actions.new_content: Seventh — With or without her consent, when she is under eighteen years of age. Age of consent raised from 16 to 18 by Criminal Law Amendment Act, 2013.

actions.old_content: shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
actions.new_content: shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than one year but which may extend to five years, and shall also be liable to fine.

amendments.title: The Criminal Law (Amendment) Act, 2018
amendments.description: Enhanced punishment for rape of children.

actions.new_content: Provided that where the victim is under twelve years of age, the minimum punishment shall be rigorous imprisonment for not less than twenty years, extendable to life imprisonment or death.

amendments.title: Jammu and Kashmir Reorganisation Act, 2019
amendments.description: Extended the IPC to Jammu and Kashmir.

actions.old_content: shall extend to the whole of India except the State of Jammu and Kashmir.
actions.new_content: shall extend to the whole of India.

rules.title: Code of Criminal Procedure — Arrest without warrant
rules.content: Any police officer may, without an order from a Magistrate and without a warrant, arrest any person who has been concerned in any cognizable offence or against whom a reasonable complaint has been made.

rules.title: Indian Evidence Act — Burden of proof in sexual offences
rules.content: Where sexual intercourse by the accused is proved and the question is whether it was without the consent of the woman alleged to have been raped, and such woman states that she did not consent, the Court shall presume that she did not consent.

rules.title: Sentencing Guidelines — Murder
rules.content: In cases of murder under Section 302, the court shall consider: (a) prior criminal record, (b) aggravating circumstances, (c) mitigating factors including age and mental state, before awarding capital punishment.
